subject: Physics
title: Spectral Lines
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Spectral Lines.

hook.text: Every element produces a unique fingerprint of colored lines when heated. No two are the same.

buildup.text: In 1859, Kirchhoff and Bunsen heated elements and passed their light through a prism.

discovery.text: Each element emitted only specific colors—bright lines at precise wavelengths. Sodium glows yellow. Hydrogen glows red.

twist.text: Stars show dark absorption lines at those same wavelengths. We can identify elements in stars trillions of miles away.

climax.text: Spectral analysis revealed helium on the Sun before anyone found it on Earth. The Sun named an element.

punchline.text: Every element has a color code. Stars can't hide their ingredients.

quiz.question: How was helium first discovered?
options: Through spectral lines observed in sunlight
